mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth signals robust economic demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings prospects improve.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 60%+ of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5% indicates tightening labor markets, which drives consumer spending and equity valuations higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when UNRATE remains below 5% for 3+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI and falling Fed Funds rates signal accommodative policy amid inflation, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when CPIAUCSL rises >0.5% and FEDFUNDS falls below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 4%, lead to higher discount rates that depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive during periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when INDPRO increases by >1% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate elevated demand for residential construction, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when HOUST increases by >10% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll gains exceeding 100k signal strong employment growth, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when PAYEMS increases by >100k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong demand pressures, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL rise by >0.3% and 0.2% MoM respectively.
